title: Whakawhanake Pūrere Auaha: Sustainable Technology Solutions for Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku
subject: Technology
culturalContext: Students will address water quality challenges facing Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku (The Dragonfly Stream), a significant waterway flowing through Ngāti Kahungunu rohe that holds cultural importance for mahinga kai (traditional food gathering) and spiritual significance. The stream's declining health impacts traditional practices and ecosystem biodiversity.
learningObjectives: Design and prototype a functional water quality monitoring device using basic electronics and programming
Analyze water quality data to identify specific contaminants affecting Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku
Develop a sustainable technology solution that integrates mātauranga Māori principles with modern engineering approaches
Present technological solutions to community stakeholders with cultural and environmental justification

activities.title: Whakawhanaungatanga ki te Awa
activities.description: Visit Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku with local kaumātua to learn about its cultural significance, historical uses, and current challenges. Students conduct initial water observations and document traditional knowledge about stream health indicators.

activities.title: Pūrere Aroturuki Wai
activities.description: Build simple water monitoring devices using Arduino microcontrollers, pH sensors, and temperature probes. Students learn basic coding to collect and transmit water quality data to digital platforms.

activities.title: Tātari Raraunga Mātauranga-rua
activities.description: Analyze collected water data alongside traditional knowledge indicators (presence of native species, water clarity traditions). Students identify patterns and potential contamination sources using both scientific and cultural frameworks.

activities.title: Whakaora te Awa
activities.description: Design and prototype intervention solutions such as native plant filtration systems, traditional weir modifications for sediment control, or digital alert systems for pollution events. Solutions must blend traditional and modern technologies.

activities.title: Whakataki Hōtaka
activities.description: Present technological solutions to community panel including Ngāti Kahungunu representatives, local farmers, and regional council staff. Demonstrate prototypes and explain how solutions honor both cultural and environmental needs.

resources: Ngāti Kahungunu kaumātua and environmental knowledge holders
Arduino starter kits with water quality sensors from Mindkits.co.nz
Regional Council water quality data for Hawke's Bay waterways
Te Ara Encyclopedia entry on traditional Māori water management practices

tasks: Functional prototype of water monitoring or improvement technology
Written report analyzing water data with cultural context
Reflection on integrating mātauranga Māori with technology design

nzcAlignment: Technological Practice: Planning for practice, brief development, outcome development and evaluation
Technological Knowledge: Understanding technological modelling and products
Nature of Technology: Characteristics of technology and characteristics of technological outcomes
Key Competencies: Participating and contributing, relating to others
